data.title: Tariff Update: Canada Maintains Reciprocal Duties on U.S., Considers Additional Measures
data.description:
Canada is maintaining its reciprocal tariffs on American imports even after U.S. President Donald Trump has delayed for one month most of the 25% import duties applied to Canadian goods.

Trump has granted a tariff exemption on goods and services from Canada and Mexico that are covered by the existing United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) on trade that was struck during the president’s first term in office.

But despite Trumpâs rollbacks, Canada is keeping its current reciprocal tariffs on $30 billion worth of U.S. goods in place. The Canadian government has also said that it stands ready to move forward with a second round of tariffs that target $125 billion of U.S. imports into the country. Trump has said that the pause on tariffs imposed on Canadian and Mexican goods will only last until April 2.

Additional Measures

Canada is also considering additional trade actions against the U.S. Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, is moving forward with plans to apply a 25% surcharge to electricity exports sent to neighboring American states such as Michigan and Wisconsin starting on March 10.

British Columbia on Canada’s west coast is planning to implement a new toll on U.S. transport trucks traveling through the province en route to Alaska. Other Canadian government officials are publicly musing about restricting potash shipments to the U.S., which is a key ingredient in fertilizer used by farmers to grow crops such as corn and soybeans.

Agriculture analysts view potash as a crucial part of the food supply. There are only a few places that produce potash and Canada is the largest global supplier. The American Farm Bureau Federation says that more than 80% of potash used in the U.S. comes from Canada. Some economists are warning about the dangers of attacking Americaâs food supply and how the Trump administration might react.

Is the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF a Buy?

The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), which tracks the movements of the benchmark U.S. stock index has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 505 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 413 Buy, 86 Hold, and six Sell recommendations made in the last three months. The average VOO price target of $639.70 implies 21.07% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Is the SEC’s Decision to Drop the Coinbase Lawsuit a Win for Crypto?
data.description:
The SEC just dropped its lawsuit against Coinbase (COIN), but not everyone is convinced it’s a win for crypto. Some are questioning whether this is a true regulatory shift or a political move tied to the industry’s hefty donations. According to Cointelegraph, Trumpâs administration has received significant financial support from crypto players like Coinbase and Uniswap (UNI-USD). So, is Washington finally warming up to crypto, or is this just payback season?

Regulatory Uncertainty Still Looms

Even with the lawsuit dismissed, crypto companies arenât in the clear just yet. The SECâs stance on token securities remains murky, and industry insiders are waiting to see if the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) takes over regulatory oversight. A shift to the CFTC could signal a more pro-crypto stance from Washington, but thereâs no guarantee.

Stablecoin Regulation Could Be a Bigger Issue

A bigger issue might be stablecoin regulation and cryptoâs access to banking. Many businesses struggle to open accounts due to perceived money laundering risks. If FinCEN updates its guidelines, banks may finally start treating crypto companies like legitimate businesses. Until then, the regulatory uncertainty continues.

Coinbaseâs Rating Update Shows Market Uncertainty

As regulations change, analysts are reassessing Coinbaseâs outlook. 5-star Barclays analyst Benjamin Budish maintained a Hold rating on COIN stock and set a price target of $328, according to TipRanks. The stock closed at $235.38 last Friday, meaning Budishâs target implies significant upside.

Other analysts are split on Coinbaseâs trajectory. The stock holds a Moderate Buy consensus with a $341.83 average price target, suggesting a nearly 60% upside. Piper Sandler also maintained a Hold rating with a $310 target. Even though Coinbase is a key player in the crypto sector, the uncertainty surrounding U.S. regulation keeps analysts cautious.

Is Coinbase a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts are cautiously bullish about COIN stock, with a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys and nine Holds. Over the past year, COIN has decreased byÂ more than 10%, and theÂ average COIN price targetÂ of $341.83 implies an upside potential of 59.6% from current levels.

See more COIN analyst ratings
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: El Salvador’s Bitcoin Buying Continues Despite IMF Loan Agreement
data.description:
El Salvador isnât backing down from Bitcoin just yet. Despite agreeing to an International Monetary Fund (IMF) loan deal that restricts government Bitcoin purchases, President Nayib Bukele says the country will keep adding to its reserves. On March 4, Bukele confirmed El Salvador had bought another Bitcoin, raising eyebrows across the industry.

IMF Agreement Puts Bitcoin Buying in Question

El Salvador struck a $1.4 billion loan agreement with the IMF in January under the condition that it would stop accumulating Bitcoin and remove its status as compulsory legal tender. However, Bukeleâs recent purchase appears to contradict that. The IMF quickly clarified that the buy didnât violate the agreement, stating it had consulted with Salvadoran officials and received assurances that purchases remained within agreed terms, according to Reuters.

Why Did El Salvador Take the Deal?

Despite Bukeleâs strong pro-Bitcoin stance, analysts suggest that economic pressure forced El Salvadorâs hand. The countryâs debt has risen significantly in recent years, and the IMF loan was a necessary step to stabilize finances. With the April 30 deadline for the agreement approaching, Bukeleâs moves suggest heâs making last-minute acquisitions before restrictions fully kick in. Whether this strategy pays off remains to be seen.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $89,361.17.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Wayfair (NYSE:W) Latest to Cut Tech Jobs as Nonfarm Payrolls Show U.S. Jobs Market Cooling
data.description:
Furniture retailer Wayfair (W) became the latest business to cut technology jobs, announcing a round of layoffs on Friday as official data showed further cooling in the U.S. labor market. In a regulatory filing posted March 7th, the e-commerce company said it would cut about 340 staff from its technology team, mirroring moves elsewhere in the tech sector. Â 

The company, which like Home Depot (HD) has battled sluggish demand for home goods amid high mortgage interest rates and a weakening consumer outlook, said it will incur charges ranging from $33 million to $38 million, primarily related to severance, benefits, and transition costs for the affected employees. Shares of Wayfair rose, chiefly helped by an upgrade to Buy from Hold at Jefferies.

U.S. Labor Market Cools

It comes amid broad signs of a cooling in the labor market. Nonfarm payrolls increased by a seasonally adjusted 151,000 on the month, less than the 170,000 forecast, the Labor Departmentâs Bureau of Labor Statistics reported Friday. The figure was up slightly on the 125,000 in January, which was revised down from the initial reading of 143,000. The unemployment rate edged up to 4.1% from 4.0% the month before.Â 

Yesterday, the Challenger jobs cut report showed U.S. employers announced 172,017 layoffs in February, up 245% from January and the highest monthly figure since July 2020, during the peak of the pandemic. More than one-third of the total came from Elon Muskâs DOGE cull of federal employees, while technology firms listed another 14,554 in cuts. 

Adding to these, Hewlett Packard EnterpriseÂ (HPE)Â said yesterday in its earnings update that it will trim its workforce by around 2,500, or 5%, as part of a cost-cutting strategy.Â 

In January, Meta Platforms (META) laid off 3,600 staff in what it said was a âlow performanceâ cull.Â Other tech firms have followed suit. Computer and printer manufacturer HP (HPQ) plans to lay off between 1,000 and 2,000 workers, whileÂ semiconductorÂ company Microchip (MCHP) said it will fire 2,000 staff and Salesforce (CRM) announced plans to cut over 1,000 jobs.Â 

Beyond the government and tech, retail is also going through retrenchments amid some soft earnings updates from the likes of Walmart (WMT), Costco (COST) and Target (TGT). According to the Challenger report, retail saw 38,956 job lossesÂ for February as companies such as Macyâs (M) and Forever 21 made sharp cuts to their headcount.

Is Wayfair a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Wayfair stock, based on 13 Buys, ten Holds and no Sells. The average W price target of $53.14 implies about 60% upside.

See more W analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: There’s a 50% Chance Bitcoin Could Surge Past $109k by June
data.description:
Bitcoinâs climb to a new all-time high of $109,000 by June isnât impossible, but it wonât be smooth sailing. According to Swan Bitcoin CEO Cory Klippsten, the chances are better than 50%, but the market needs time to absorb macroeconomic turbulence. “The market needs to first digest tariffs, trade war fears, and growth scare fears. Bitcoin trading below $100,000 right now feels like a pause, not an end to the bull run,” Klippsten told Cointelegraph.

Trumpâs Policies Keep Traders on Edge

Bitcoinâs recent sell-off hasnât gone unnoticed. The cryptocurrency has dropped nearly 14% since President Donald Trump announced new import tariffs on China, Canada, and Mexico. And while the newly signed Strategic Bitcoin Reserve executive order is a historic step, it didnât deliver the immediate buying pressure that traders were hoping for. Instead of fresh acquisitions, the reserve will only hold Bitcoin confiscated by the U.S. government in criminal cases, leaving many wondering when (or if) additional purchases will happen.

Bitcoin Expected to Bounce Between $85K and $95K

Bitcoin is now consolidating after a sharp drop to $84,700, and analysts predict it could hover between $85,000 and $95,000 for the next several weeks. Network economist Timothy Peterson told Cointelegraph that this phase is likely just a cooldown before Bitcoin trends back over $100,000 later in the year. Meanwhile, Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley isnât too worried, pointing out that Bitcoin also dipped after spot ETFs launched in Januaryâonly to surge to new highs later.

U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Could Boost Market Cap by $460 Billion

Despite short-term uncertainty, analysts say a national Bitcoin reserve could eventually add billions in market value. Sygnum Bank estimates that a government-led crypto stockpile could boost Bitcoinâs market cap by 25%, triggering massive institutional inflows. “Because the liquid supply of Bitcoin is very small, larger inflows cause upward price shocks,” Sygnumâs research head Katalin Tischhauser told Cointelegraph. If federal or state governments start accumulating, the impact could be seismic.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $89,384.62.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Trump’s Bitcoin Reserve Snubs XRP but Bulls Keep Buying
data.description:
President Donald Trumpâs long-awaited Strategic Bitcoin Reserve is official, but thereâs a catchâXRP (XRP-USD) and other altcoins didnât make the cut. The executive order, signed Thursday, only includes Bitcoin seized from criminal cases, shutting out new acquisitions. Thatâs not what the market wanted to hear. Coinbase (COIN) CEO Brian Armstrong, Ripple executives, and other crypto leaders have weighed in, with traders scrambling to adjust their bets. According to CoinDesk, traders are rushing to hedge against downside risk in Bitcoin (BTC-USD), Ethereum (ETH-USD), and Solana (SOL-USD), snapping up short-term put options to protect against potential losses. Meanwhile, XRP options have been an exception, showing stronger demand for bullish call options over longer timeframes.

Markets Adjust to Bitcoin-Only Reserve

The absence of new purchases means the reserve serves merely as a strategic stockpile, with no fresh buying pressure on the market. That has disappointed traders, leading to a surge in short-term put options for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana, according to Deribit data tracked by Block Scholes. This reflects growing concerns over near-term price volatility. However, XRP has bucked the trend, with options data showing demand for call optionsâsuggesting traders see upside potential.

Traders Await Clarity from the White House Crypto Summit

Investors are now looking to todayâs White House Crypto Summit, hoping for clearer regulatory guidelines. Bitget Researchâs chief analyst, Ryan Lee, noted that potential announcements on token classification, tax incentives, and enforcement policies could influence institutional sentiment. Meanwhile, the February U.S. jobs report, also due today, may introduce another layer of market volatility.

Bitcoin Bounces Despite Disappointment

Despite initial disappointment, Bitcoin has already clawed back some losses. After dipping to $84,713 following the announcement, BTC rebounded 4%, trading near $88,982. According to Cointelegraph, seasoned traders see the marketâs reaction as overblown, with some analysts calling this a âclassic overreactionâ before a stronger recovery.

Overall, XRP has stood out as a rare bullish outlier, but the real test will be the outcomes of the White House Crypto Summit. Whether Trumpâs policy stance will shift or stay the course remains to be seen.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $2.5086.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) Bulls Circle the Wagons amid Elon Musk’s Political Controversies
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock may be down by a third in 2025 on faltering sales and concerns about its CEO Elon Musk taking his eye off the ball, but the pullback is creating opportunities for investors, according to resolute bulls on Wall Street.

TD Cowen upgraded Tesla to Buy from Hold with aÂ price targetÂ of $388, up from $180. The firm cameÂ out âtactically bullishâ given the recent share price pullback coming âahead of several potentially consequential catalysts this year.â Analyst Jeff Osborne says Teslaâs âpotentially game-changing level catalystsâ across electric vehicles, autonomous vehicles and robotics are enough to support the stock this year.Â 

It comes as Teslaâs EV sales in Europe crumble amid Muskâs cozying up to President Donald Trump and increasingly embittered relations between the White House and European nations over issues such as Ukraine and trade. Meanwhile, Muskâs support for hard-right parties in Europe, such as Germanyâs AfD, has irked many. Teslaâs sales in the first two months of the year are down 71% in Germany and 44% in France.

Musk has also led efforts at the new Department of Government Efficiency to reduce costs, leading to a sharp rise in layoffs last month in the U.S.Â The Wall Street Journal ran with a story yesterday on how Musk’s politics were eroding the Tesla brand’s appeal among core buyers of EVs.

TSLA Bulls Out in ForceÂ 

Despite or perhaps because of some of these concerns some notable Tesla bulls have come out with upbeat notes in recent days.  

In a note this week, Tesla cheerleader Dan Ives at Wedbush addressed DOGE and the political controversies, saying theseÂ have minimal impact on Teslaâs sales. He estimates that less than 5% of Teslaâs global sales are at risk due to these factors, despite plenty of negative sentiment.Â 

In fact, Ives even added Tesla to the “Best Ideas List” at Wedbush while reiterating Street-high $550 price target. 

Mmeanwhile, Morgan Stanleyâs Adam JonasÂ praised Teslaâs potential in the evolving landscape ofÂ AIÂ and robotics to offset the companyâs struggles in theÂ EVÂ market as he reinstated the stock as the âTop Pickâ in U.S. autos.Â 

Jonas, who upped his price target to $430, said Teslaâs recent travails were âemblematic of a company in the transition from an automotive âpure playâ to a highly diversified play on AI and robotics.â

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street analysts continue to hold a neutral view on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks,Â TSLA stockÂ has a Hold consensus rating, with 14 Buys, 11 Holds, and 10 Sells assigned in the last three months. TheÂ average price target for Tesla shares is $351.48, suggesting a potential upside of about 33% from the current level.

See more TSLA analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: Crypto & Stock Markets Shake as Interest Rate Fears Overshadow Tariff Drama
data.description:
President Donald Trump may have softened his stance on tariffs, but markets arenât biting. On Thursday, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick confirmed that Mexico would be exempt from Trumpâs newly announced 25% tariffsâinitially sending Bitcoin soaring past $91,000. But the relief didnât last. Stocks tumbled, Bitcoin slipped back to $88,500, and investors quickly turned their attention to a bigger concern: rising interest rates.

Stagflation Fears Hit Risk Assets

While Washington was busy with tariff negotiations, global bond yields were skyrocketing. Germany saw one of its worst bond crashes ever, with 10-year Bund yields jumping over 40 basis points to 2.83%. Meanwhile, Japanâs 10-year government bond yield more than doubled over the past six months, now sitting at 1.51%.

The U.S. hasnât been spared. After declining sharply since Trumpâs inauguration, the 10-year Treasury yield spiked over 20 basis points in just 48 hours, reaching 4.30%. According to Lekker Capitalâs Quinn Thompson, this kind of rate surge amid slowing growth is a textbook case of stagflation. âHistorically, this has not treated risk assets well,â he warned.

Investors Should Wait for Jobs Report

The February U.S. Nonfarm Payrolls Report drops on Friday, and it could be a game-changer. Economists expect 160,000 new jobs, up from 143,000 in January, with unemployment holding steady at 4%. But if the number beats expectations, interest rates could surge even higherâputting more pressure on stocks and crypto alike.

Investors can track how these developments impact crypto prices on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Trump Should Sort Boeing (NYSE:BA) Delivery Mess says Germany’s Lufthansa
data.description:
German flag carrier Lufthansa (DE:LHA) is hopeful the Trump administration will spur Boeing (BA) to overcome problems that have led to a major industry-wide headache; delayed delivery of jets.Â 

Boeingâs well publicized delivery delays have been a bone of contention in the airline industry for some time. Itâs been just over a year since a door panel blew off a Boeing 737 Max 9 operated by Alaska Airlines, prompting all kinds of questions about build quality and safety and leading to a number of changes at the company amid intense scrutiny. There have also been two fatal crashes and in May the Federal Aviation Administration opened an investigation into theÂ 787 DreamlinerÂ amid allegations of inspection shortcuts. A seven-week strike by Boeing machinists that ended in November also impacted deliveries.Â 

RyanairÂ laid the blame on the aircraft manufacturer for having to repeatedly lower its capacity guidance this year. In October, the head of theÂ International Air Transport Association said delays at Boeing and rival Airbus (EADSF)Â were “massively frustrating” and are not getting better.

Lufthansa Sees Change Ahead at BA

ButÂ Lufthansa CEO Carsten Spohr is hopeful that things will change in 2025. He told CNBC that heâs hoping issues leading to delays such as supply chain troubles and problems relating to FAA certification will be overcome âwith some support of the new U.S. administration.âÂ 

In addition to the eponymous flag-carrying Lufthansa Airlines, Lufthansa Group also operates Eurowings, Austrian Airlines, Brussels Airlines, ITA Airways and Swiss International Air Lines. Itâs grappling with an ageing fleet with an average aircraft age of 14 years, up from 13.4 years in the last 12 months. 

Lufthansa has about 240 jets on order for the coming years, but almost all will face delay. At the end of 2024, the Lufthansa Group fleet comprised 735Â aircraft, split between Airbus and Boeing birds. A total of 18Â new aircraft were added to the fleet last year, but only one was from Boeing.

Airlines Look to Recover in 2025 after Tough Year

Spohrâs remarks came as Lufthansa reported a â¬948 million decline in earnings, partly due to having to maintain older planes as a result of delayed deliveries, as well as strikes and mounting price pressures. However, shares soared as the earnings were not as bad as feared and the company issued an upbeat outlook for the year ahead.  

Boeingâs European rival Airbus is also struggling with supply chain problems that have been impacting the airline industry for years. Deliveries from Airbus are down 18% this year, partly due to it pullingÂ forward deliveries from engine maker CFM to meet its 2024 annual targets.Â 

Tariffs may add to airline industry problems in 2025. Airbus has said it could prioritize deliveries to its non-U.S. customers in the event of tariff disruption. However, despite souring relations between Brussels and Washington, demand from American tourists is growing and Lufthansa believes it can benefit. Air France-KLM also pointed to U.S. being a key factor in its recovery from a challenging year in 2024 after it too reported a steep fall in profits this week. Â 

âOf course, tariffs will not be supporting global trade so letâs hope that can be avoided, but nevertheless my optimism is partly fueled this year by very strong transatlantic travel on both sides of the Atlantic,â Spohr added in his CNBC interview.Â 

Is BA a Good Stock to Buy? 

Wall Street analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 13 Buys, five Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months. TheÂ average BA price targetÂ of $196.88 per share implies about 24% upside potential.

See more BA analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Making U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Official Policy
data.description:
Itâs officialâPresident Donald Trump has signed an executive order establishing a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. This is a huge moment of progress for U.S. crypto policy. According to White House AI and Crypto Czar David Sacks, the reserve will be funded using Bitcoin seized in government criminal cases. “The Reserve will be capitalized with Bitcoin owned by the federal government that was forfeited as part of criminal or civil asset forfeiture proceedings,” Sacks stated in a March 7 X post.

Bitcoin Becomes the Digital Fort Knox

The move cements Bitcoinâs role as a store of value, with Sacks calling it “a digital Fort Knox.” Unlike other government-held assets that could be liquidated, the Bitcoin placed in the reserve will not be sold, ensuring it remains a long-term strategic asset. The White House added that the Treasury and Commerce departments will develop “budget-neutral strategies” to buy additional Bitcoin without burdening taxpayers.

Additionally, for the first time, the government will conduct a full audit of its crypto holdings. Sacks confirmed that agencies must provide a detailed report of their digital asset holdings to the Treasury Secretary and Trumpâs crypto task force.

What about Altcoins?

Alongside the Bitcoin reserve, Trumpâs order also creates a U.S. Digital Asset Stockpile, which will hold cryptocurrencies other than Bitcoinâalso sourced from government forfeitures. Unlike the Bitcoin reserve, the stockpileâs assets could be sold off if the Treasury Secretary deems it necessary.

Trump previously hinted that XRP (XRP-USD), SOL (SOL-USD), and ADA (ADA-USD) would be part of the reserve. However, Arkham Intelligence data shows the U.S. government does not currently hold these assets. So now, naturally, people are raising questions about how they would be acquired. Meanwhile, the U.S. already controls 198,109 Bitcoin, worth roughly $17.87 billion, alongside $119 million in Ethereum.

White House Crypto Summit Takes Place Today

Today is the day of the White House Crypto Summit. Whether this event leads to concrete policy changes or just fuels further speculation remains to be seen. One thing is certainâcrypto markets will react to whatever unfolds. Investors looking to stay informed should monitor crypto price movements in theÂ TipRanksÂ CryptocurrencyÂ Center. Click the image below for more details.
data.author: Annika Masrani
